items.title: Serotonergic modulation of the BNST–CeA pathway reveals sex differences in fear learning
items.author: Nesha S. Burghardt
items.content: Ravenelle et al. show that systematically increasing serotonin has sex-specific effects on fear learning in mice. In females only, increasing serotonin in the BNST enhances fear memory by promoting communication with the amygdala.

items.title: Hypoxia ameliorates neurodegeneration and movement disorder in a mouse model of Parkinson’s disease
items.author: Fumito Ichinose
items.content: Parkinson’s disease (PD) involves toxic protein buildup and energy failure in neurons. Continuously breathing low-oxygen air protected mice from PD-like neuronal loss and reversed symptoms, even after they began, suggesting that hypoxia may protect neurons.

items.title: Alzheimer’s disease transcriptional landscape in ex vivo human microglia
items.author: Panos Roussos
items.content: Here the authors provide a comprehensive transcriptomic dataset of human primary microglia for Alzheimer’s disease and healthy aging. They identify dysregulation of immune-related microglial functions as a hallmark of disease.

items.title: The psychoplastogen tabernanthalog induces neuroplasticity without proximate immediate early gene activation
items.author: David E. Olson
items.content: Aarrestad et al. show that, in contrast to psychedelics, the nonhallucinogenic psychoplastogen tabernanthalog promotes neuroplasticity through 5-HT2AR activation without immediately increasing extracellular glutamate levels or immediate early gene expression.

items.title: An integrated single-nucleus and spatial transcriptomics atlas reveals the molecular landscape of the human hippocampus
items.author: Stephanie C. Hicks
items.content: The topographical organization of cells in the hippocampus reflects its ability to regulate mood and cognition. Here the authors generate a spatially resolved gene expression map in the human hippocampus to enable cross-species and functional interpretation.
